body.title: tmux nag on new/reopened alarm (out-of-band) for workers running in tmux
body.intention: I intend that a worker running in tmux gets an unignorable in-session nag when a new/reopened alarm appears, pointing it at the alarms.
body.description:
**Problem:** a worker heads-down in a tmux session won't see a web panel. It needs an in-session nudge on a NEW/reopened alarm (not per-increment — calm signals).

**Shape:** An out-of-band notifier (hook or periodic sweep) detects new/reopened open alarms and, when a worker is running in tmux, sends a tmux send-keys nag pointing at the alarms (reuse the NotificationDispatcher LOUD/tmux pattern). One nag per new/reopened signature, never per dedup increment. Out-of-band so it never touches the raise_alarm hot path.

**Affected files:** a small notifier module (reads system_alarms store), reuses unseen_university/notification_dispatcher.py tmux path.
**Design rules:** out-of-band (not in raise_alarm); calm-signals (one nag per new/reopened, not per increment); fail-soft.
**Scope boundary:** IN — detect new/reopened + tmux nag when in tmux. OUT — the web panel (T-system-alarms-web-panel); choosing alarm sites.
**Test plan:** a first-seen alarm yields one nag (tmux path invoked once); a 2nd increment yields none; with no tmux session, the notifier degrades quietly (no nag, no error).
**Completion criteria:** new/reopened alarm → exactly one tmux nag when a session is present; increment → zero; absent tmux session → silent no-op, never raises.

body.result: Out-of-band notify_new_alarms nags once per new/reopened alarm via tmux send-keys (points at 'uu alarms'); increments don't re-nag, reopens do; absent tmux = silent retry-next-sweep no-op. Proof: tests/test_system_alarm_notifier.py @ 9a6fca03 (stub-first red→green).
